DenoiseRep is a denoising module that removes noise from features during inference. It shows better performance with small-scale backbones compared to large-scale backbones due to the noise contained in features.
Methods using the plugin outperform original methods with the same backbone on all datasets. DenoiseRep enhances performance by removing noise from features.
Our method achieves almost best performance on ViT-base backbone with a large number of parameters.
Adding DenoiseRep to a poorly performing backbone might show more significant performance improvement. Further verification is required for this claim.
DenoiseRep is a denoising ability that has been verified in the currently optimal ReID method.
DenoiseRep is a computation-free method that employs a denoising layer into each feature layer for fusion.
DenoiseRep is compared in Parameter Fusion Performance Analysis for denoising based on the features of the final layer or based on the features of each layer.
DenoiseRep− is added to the baseline method TransReID-SSL, resulting in an improvement in performance.
DenoiseRepDukeMTMC MSMT17 Market1501 CUHK-03 shows varied performance in Inference Time compared to the baseline method TransReID-SSL.

Adding DenoiseRep− to the baseline method TransReID-SSL is able to improve performance, showing the effectiveness of feature-based denoising. However, it also introduces extra inference latency of about 15% due to adding an extra parameter-independent denoising module.
Adding DenoiseRep− increases the inference latency by about 15% as it includes an extra denoising module at the end of the model.
The operation of fusion allows merging the parameters of the denoising module with the original parameters, leading to increased efficiency without extra inference latency costs.
The DenoiseRep is based on denoising at the feature level and shows strong generalization capabilities, as demonstrated in experiments on different vision tasks.
